name: golembot
description: Run Cursor, Claude Code, OpenCode, or Codex on Slack, Telegram, Discord, Feishu, DingTalk, WeCom — or embed into any Node.js product.

commit.message: docs: update README slogan to match homepage

chore(release): 0.26.0 [skip ci]

# [0.26.0](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/compare/v0.25.0...v0.26.0) (2026-03-11)

### Bug Fixes

* **workspace:** restore missing closing brace in inbox config parsing ([f0fc611](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/f0fc611f2ffe4f78c117d5864ce39f14d4d0f636)), closes [#14](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/issues/14)

### Features

* **provider:** decouple engine from LLM provider via env injection ([c80a362](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/c80a3628bf669460d0e5085f5e2c31e940e88cb3))

fix(workspace): restore missing closing brace in inbox config parsing

The provider PR (#14) accidentally removed the closing `}` for the
`if (doc.inbox)` block, causing `pnpm build` to fail with TS1005.

feat(provider): decouple engine from LLM provider via env injection

Add ProviderConfig to golem.yaml that lets users route any engine
(Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode) to a third-party LLM API
(e.g. MiniMax, DeepSeek, OpenRouter) without modifying the CLI's
global config. Provider settings are injected as env vars at spawn time.

Key changes:
- ProviderConfig type with per-engine model overrides and Codex profile
  support (codexProfile, codexProviderId, codexWireApi, codexEnvKey)
- Per-engine env mapping functions (provider-env.ts)
- Claude Code: use ANTHROPIC_MODEL env var instead of --model flag in
  provider mode; add --setting-sources project,local to isolate from
  user-level settings; improved error diagnostics (result.errors, stderr)
- Codex: native profile/provider support via -c flags
- Provider presets for common providers (provider-presets.ts)
- Engine discovery API (discoverEngines)
- Assistant.setProvider() and Assistant.discoverEngines() public API
- Comprehensive documentation (docs/guide/provider-routing.md)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

global config. Provider settings are injected as env vars at spawn time.

Key changes:

- Claude Code: use ANTHROPIC_MODEL env var instead of --model flag in

# [0.25.0](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/compare/v0.24.2...v0.25.0) (2026-03-11)

### Bug Fixes

* **feishu:** correct fetchHistory timestamp parsing — create_time is already milliseconds ([a42996c](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/a42996c3373105710cb7be0c4a2fd7959a3c0324))
* **inbox:** preserve mentioned field, fix dedup key to use channelType, and avoid watermark boundary duplicates ([2ef8ad3](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/2ef8ad3bd2f60b8a804bcda740ee0d6a0c9ca223))
* **slack:** pass explicit token in listChats/fetchHistory and gracefully degrade without groups:read scope ([a579e3d](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/a579e3d335f17005df3c527e9e6a206995061f98))

### Features

* **gateway:** add [SKIP] sentinel for history-fetch triage — bot can stay silent when no reply needed ([849a7e8](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/849a7e8de55e031c7866a261681baf2d7e2b7d93))
* **inbox:** add persistent message queue and historical message fetching ([fd1cb39](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/fd1cb39baf4935f8e3363bdca73f9c94c6896626)), closes [hi#water](https://github.com/hi/issues/water)

docs: restructure sidebar into Guide + Features, add group-chat and inbox pages

Split the sidebar into two modules:
- Guide (4 items): Getting Started, Configuration, CLI Commands, Docker
- Features (5 items): Group Chat, Inbox & History Fetch, Scheduled Tasks, Memory, Embed

New pages consolidate content previously scattered across multiple files:
- guide/group-chat.md — response policies, mentions, quote reply, group memory
- guide/inbox.md — persistent queue, history fetch, smart triage, platform support

Slimmed down configuration.md, channels/overview.md, and memory.md by replacing
extracted content with brief summaries and cross-links.
fix(slack): pass explicit token in listChats/fetchHistory and gracefully degrade without groups:read scope
fix(inbox): preserve mentioned field, fix dedup key to use channelType, and avoid watermark boundary duplicates
feat(gateway): add [SKIP] sentinel for history-fetch triage — bot can stay silent when no reply needed
fix(feishu): correct fetchHistory timestamp parsing — create_time is already milliseconds

feat(inbox): add persistent message queue and historical message fetching

Implement "digital employee" capabilities:

Phase 1 — Persistent Inbox (InboxStore):
- JSONL-backed message queue (.golem/inbox.jsonl) replaces fire-and-forget
- Sequential consumer loop processes messages one by one (like a human)
- Crash recovery: processing entries restored to pending on restart
- No message rejection under high concurrency — all messages enqueued
- Configurable via inbox.enabled / inbox.retentionDays in golem.yaml

Phase 2 — Historical Message Fetching:
- ChannelAdapter interface extended with fetchHistory() + listChats()
- Implemented for Feishu (im.v1.message.list), Slack (conversations.history),
  Discord (channel.messages.fetch)
- WatermarkStore tracks per-chat high-water mark to avoid re-fetching
- Smart triage: batches missed messages into a single prompt for the agent
  to decide which to reply, skip, or batch-respond
- Periodic polling as safety net against WebSocket disconnections
- Configurable via historyFetch.enabled / pollIntervalMinutes / initialLookbackMinutes

## [0.24.1](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/compare/v0.24.0...v0.24.1) (2026-03-10)

### Bug Fixes

* **slack:** pre-validate auth token before starting Socket Mode ([2e34474](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/2e3447437d4c7f6be0e5597d56e4638ff240cf48))

fix(slack): pre-validate auth token before starting Socket Mode

Calling auth.test before app.start() so invalid tokens fail fast
with a clear error instead of crashing the entire gateway process.

chore(release): 0.24.0 [skip ci]

# [0.24.0](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/compare/v0.23.0...v0.24.0) (2026-03-10)

### Features

* **channels:** add quote reply and cross-platform [@mention](https://github.com/mention) support ([5d7168a](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/5d7168a6762038dfeb42467823d5285df7fcec50))

feat(channels): add quote reply and cross-platform @mention support

- Add messageId to ChannelMessage for quote/reply across all 6 adapters
- Feishu: use im.v1.message.reply for native quote reply
- Telegram: use reply_to_message_id for native quote reply
- Slack: use thread_ts for thread reply, add getGroupMembers() for @mention
- Discord: add getGroupMembers() for @mention, native message.reply()
- Fix Slack mention escaping: substitute after markdownToMrkdwn conversion
- Fix gateway coordinator TDZ bug causing crash on early message arrival
- Add scheduled tasks standalone docs (EN + ZH)
- Update channel overview docs with quote reply and mention tables

fix: add missing listHistoryFiles and readHistory exports

The `golembot log` command (cli.ts) imports `listHistoryFiles` and
`readHistory` from session.ts, but these functions were never
implemented. This causes a runtime error when running `golembot log`.

Add both functions to session.ts:
- listHistoryFiles(dir): reads .golem/history/ directory, returns
  session keys from .jsonl filenames
- readHistory(dir, sessionKey, limit?): reads JSONL history file,
  parses entries, supports tail limiting

Includes unit tests for both functions (5 new test cases).

docs(zh): translate reference/ and testing/ documentation

Translate 9 documentation files to Chinese:

Reference (7 files):
- architecture.md
- coding-cli-cursor.md
- coding-cli-claude-code.md
- coding-cli-opencode.md
- coding-cli-codex.md
- coding-cli-comparison.md
- coding-cli-docs.md

Testing (2 files):
- im-playbook.md
- issues.md

Translation follows existing zh/ conventions: code blocks, CLI
commands, config keys, and URLs preserved in English. Technical
terms use established translations (engine=引擎, session=会话,
skill=技能, etc.).

docs(todo): mark fullText, status, and permissions as completed

Mark four TODO items as done following PRs #6, #7, and #8:

- bugs-and-debt.md: P2 result.result Field Not Exposed (fullText)
- bugs-and-debt.md: P2 Permissions Not Utilized
- enhancements.md: P2 Permissions Integration
- enhancements.md: P2 golembot status

chore: add Biome linter, formatter, and Husky pre-commit hooks

- Configure Biome v2 as linter + formatter (2-space indent, single quotes, semicolons, 120 line width)
- Auto-format all 61 source files for consistent style
- Fix unused imports and variables across codebase
- Add lint-staged to run Biome on staged .ts files before commit
- Add CI lint step in GitHub Actions workflow
- Add npm scripts: lint, lint:fix, format
docs: update docs for fullText, session count, and permissions features

# [0.23.0](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/compare/v0.22.0...v0.23.0) (2026-03-10)

### Features

* **cli:** add session count to golembot status ([#7](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/issues/7)) ([2c149e0](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/2c149e0f80403f8ad868e4c6e889dadde14bd190))
* **engine:** expose fullText on done StreamEvent ([#6](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/issues/6)) ([739323c](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/739323c250d3969adf77761aba5634aae5f16c17))
* **workspace:** add permissions config with .cursor/cli.json generation ([#8](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/issues/8)) ([b5c59a2](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/b5c59a2c1e68934f7cf522fc7fe8fd0148650ba3))

feat(workspace): add permissions config with .cursor/cli.json generation (#8)

Add a permissions section to golem.yaml that controls what the agent is
allowed to do (paths and commands). When permissions are configured:

1. initWorkspace generates .cursor/cli.json with the mapped rules
2. CursorEngine skips --trust so the CLI enforces granular permissions
3. Useful for CI/CD security (restricting file write scope, commands)

This resolves the 'P2: Permissions Integration' TODO from
docs/todo/enhancements.md.

golem.yaml example:
  permissions:
    allowedPaths: ['./src', './tests']
    deniedPaths: ['./.env', './secrets']
    allowedCommands: ['npm test']
    deniedCommands: ['rm -rf *']

Changes:
- src/workspace.ts: PermissionsConfig type, loadConfig/writeConfig/init
  support, generateCursorCliJson function
- src/engine.ts: add hasPermissionsConfig to InvokeOpts
- src/engines/cursor.ts: conditionally omit --trust when permissions set
- src/index.ts: pass hasPermissionsConfig to engine invoke
- src/__tests__/workspace.test.ts: 8 new tests for permissions

Co-authored-by: GitHub User <user@example.com>

commit.message:
feat(engine): expose fullText on done StreamEvent (#6)

Add fullText?: string to the done StreamEvent type. When the Cursor or
Claude Code engine emits a non-error result event, the agent's final
result text (obj.result) is now forwarded as fullText on the done event.

This resolves the TODO item 'P2: Expose fullText field' from
docs/todo/enhancements.md. The change is minimal and backward-compatible:
existing consumers that do not read fullText are unaffected.

Changes:
- src/engine.ts: add fullText to StreamEvent done variant
- src/engines/cursor.ts: extract obj.result into fullText
- src/engines/claude-code.ts: extract obj.result into fullText
- src/__tests__/engine.test.ts: update assertions to cover fullText

Co-authored-by: GitHub User <user@example.com>

commit.message:
feat(cli): add session count to golembot status (#7)

Add countSessions() to the session module and use it in the status
command to display the number of tracked sessions. This resolves the
remaining piece of the 'P2: golembot status' TODO from
docs/todo/enhancements.md — the command already showed name, engine,
skills, channels, and gateway info, but was missing the session count.

Changes:
- src/session.ts: export countSessions(dir) → number
- src/cli.ts: status command now shows Sessions line (human + JSON)
- src/__tests__/session.test.ts: add countSessions unit tests (3 cases)

Co-authored-by: GitHub User <user@example.com>

commit.message: docs: add image multimodal support documentation (EN + ZH)

chore(release): 0.22.0 [skip ci]

# [0.22.0](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/compare/v0.21.0...v0.22.0) (2026-03-09)

### Features

* add image multimodal message support across all IM channels ([99742c0](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/99742c0b849f78af4acbec0b5e45eeef7c64e52d))

feat: add image multimodal message support across all IM channels

- Add ImageAttachment type to ChannelMessage for carrying image data
- Feishu: handle image/post message types, download via IM resource API
- Telegram: download photos via Bot API (largest size)
- Discord: download image attachments from CDN
- Slack: download file uploads with Bearer token auth
- DingTalk: handle picture/richText message types
- WeCom: download images via media API
- Gateway: pass images through to assistant, allow image-only messages
- HTTP API: accept base64-encoded images in POST /chat body
- Assistant: save images to .golem/images/, append paths to prompt,
  pass imagePaths in InvokeOpts, clean up after response
- Add im-image-test.ts example for manual IM testing

chore(release): 0.21.0 [skip ci]

# [0.21.0](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/compare/v0.20.0...v0.21.0) (2026-03-09)

### Features

* add scheduled tasks panel to Dashboard with disable/enable/run controls ([39c3d40](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/39c3d40ffc62fcea24298988e4bb7a76340196e4))

feat: add scheduled tasks panel to Dashboard with disable/enable/run controls

Dashboard now shows a "Cron Tasks" section when tasks are configured:
- Task list with name, schedule, target channel, enabled status, last run
- Run Now / Enable / Disable buttons with live result display
- Recent execution history per task (time, status, duration, cost)
- /cron commands now resolve task name → id for better usability

chore(release): 0.20.0 [skip ci]

# [0.20.0](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/compare/v0.19.0...v0.20.0) (2026-03-09)

### Features

* add scheduled task system with cron scheduler and proactive messaging ([0b48e2a](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/0b48e2abe712f0faccf55d659dcb93fe7bfb6b60))

feat: add scheduled task system with cron scheduler and proactive messaging

Zero-dependency cron parser + setTimeout chain scheduler for running
agent tasks on schedule. Tasks defined in golem.yaml are executed by
the agent and results pushed to IM channels via new adapter.send().

- Scheduler with standard cron + human-readable formats (every 30m, daily 09:00)
- TaskStore for file-based persistence (.golem/tasks.json + history JSONL)
- ProactiveCoordinator connecting scheduler + assistant + channel adapters
- adapter.send() on Feishu, Slack, Telegram, Discord, WeCom
- /cron slash commands (list/run/enable/disable/del/history) across CLI, HTTP, IM
- golem.yaml tasks config with mergeConfigTasks for restart resilience
- Bilingual docs (EN+ZH): configuration, CLI, HTTP API, channel-adapter, overview, homepage

chore(release): 0.19.0 [skip ci]

# [0.19.0](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/compare/v0.18.1...v0.19.0) (2026-03-09)

### Bug Fixes

* reset model when switching engines and fix patchConfig safety ([5e51c00](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/5e51c000e21c55a209db28ffc0a7b1962bf98c18))

### Features

* add /model list command with real-time model discovery ([a55a3b7](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/a55a3b7296ab74894c2d4f962d6d922e4e1562e5))
* add unified slash commands across CLI, HTTP API, and IM gateway ([e6b9a85](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/e6b9a8528b03c95e0d3b79add9200df943c9e681))
Merge branch 'beta'

feat: add /model list command with real-time model discovery

Each engine fetches models from its native source:
- Claude Code: Anthropic API /v1/models
- Codex: OpenAI API /v1/models
- OpenCode: OpenRouter API or CLI fallback
- Cursor: agent --list-models CLI

Also updates docs (EN + ZH) with full slash command reference.

fix: reset model when switching engines and fix patchConfig safety

- /engine now clears the model override since model formats differ between
  engines (e.g. opencode uses "openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5",
  claude-code uses "claude-sonnet-4-6")
- setEngine() accepts clearModel flag to atomically patch both fields
- Fix arrow function wrappers in server.ts, gateway.ts, cli.ts that were
  silently dropping the clearModel parameter
- Rewrite patchConfig to use regex-based text replacement instead of
  yaml.load+yaml.dump, which was corrupting ${ENV_VAR} placeholders and
  producing duplicate YAML entries

feat: add unified slash commands across CLI, HTTP API, and IM gateway

Implement /help, /status, /engine, /model, /skill, /reset as built-in
commands that work consistently across all three interfaces. Commands are
defined in a shared module (commands.ts) with a CommandContext/CommandResult
pattern — each interface provides its own context and renders results
appropriately.

Key changes:
- New src/commands.ts: shared command parsing and execution
- Assistant interface: add setEngine(), setModel(), getStatus() methods
- workspace.ts: add patchConfig() for safe runtime config updates
- gateway.ts: slash command interception in IM message handler
- server.ts: slash command interception in POST /chat endpoint
- cli.ts: replace hardcoded /help and /reset with shared module
- dashboard.ts: async buildDashboardData() with live engine/model status
- 24 new tests covering commands, gateway integration, dashboard async
Merge branch 'beta'

# [0.18.0-beta.1](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/compare/v0.17.4...v0.18.0-beta.1) (2026-03-09)

### Features

* streaming message delivery and Feishu read receipts ([7208ee3](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/7208ee3c0bb30c0341c6ccc88732974051dc8268))

feat: streaming message delivery and Feishu read receipts

Add streaming mode for IM channels that sends messages incrementally
using semantic boundary flushing (paragraph breaks, tool_call events)
instead of accumulating a single giant reply. Configurable via
golem.yaml `streaming.mode` and `streaming.showToolCalls`.

Add Feishu read receipt tracking via im.message.message_read_v1 event
subscription for future proactive messaging support.

Tested with Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex engines on real Feishu bot.

fix: reload .env before starting gateway from onboard

The CLI-level .env loader runs at startup before onboard creates the
.env file, so channel tokens are missing from process.env when the
gateway starts. Re-load the newly created .env right before launching.

chore(release): 0.14.0-beta.10 [skip ci]

# [0.14.0-beta.10](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/compare/v0.14.0-beta.9...v0.14.0-beta.10) (2026-03-08)

### Bug Fixes

* use importPeer() to resolve channel SDKs from bot directory ([be9ff1e](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/be9ff1e51df883745a3007fc826da65f38790279))
Merge branch 'beta'

fix: use importPeer() to resolve channel SDKs from bot directory

The previous preloadChannelDeps approach was broken: import() caches
modules by resolved specifier, so pre-importing via absolute path does
NOT make a subsequent bare import('grammy') succeed.

New approach: importPeer() helper (using documented createRequire API)
that each adapter calls instead of bare import(). Falls back to bot
working directory when global resolution fails.

### Bug Fixes

* use createRequire to resolve channel deps from bot directory ([fdf50b2](https://github.com/0xranx/golembot/commit/fdf50b289df5a9bd54a65d02f92dee5b13afbbd1))
Merge branch 'beta'

fix: use createRequire to resolve channel deps from bot directory

Replace unstable Module._initPaths() hack with documented createRequire() API.
Before each adapter starts, preloadChannelDeps() resolves peer-deps from the
bot node_modules via createRequire(), then import() caches them so adapter
code finds them naturally.

### Bug Fixes

Merge branch 'beta'

fix: resolve channel peer-deps from bot working directory

When GolemBot is installed globally, dynamic import() for channel
SDKs (grammy, discord.js, etc.) resolves from GolemBot's install
path, not the bot's node_modules. Add bot dir to NODE_PATH so
deps installed via onboard are found at gateway startup.

readme_html:
English | 中文

Cursor, Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex — these Coding Agents can already write code, run scripts, analyze data, and reason through complex tasks. But they're stuck in an IDE or a terminal window.

GolemBot gives them a body. One command connects your Coding Agent to Slack, Telegram, Discord, Feishu, DingTalk, WeCom, or any HTTP client — with any LLM provider. Route Claude Code through OpenRouter, run Codex on MiniMax, or point OpenCode at DeepSeek — one config block, zero code changes. Write a custom adapter to plug in email, GitHub Issues, or any other message source. Or embed into your own product with 5 lines of code. No AI framework, no prompt engineering — the agent you already have is the brain.

Run Your Coding Agent Everywhere

On IM — your team's 24/7 AI teammate

golembot init -e claude-code -n my-bot
golembot gateway    # Slack, Telegram, Discord, Feishu, DingTalk, WeCom

Your colleagues @ the bot in group chat. It can write code, analyze files, answer questions — because behind it is a real Coding Agent, not a thin API wrapper.

In your product — full agent power, 5 lines of code

import { createAssistant } from 'golembot';
const bot = createAssistant({ dir: './my-agent' });

for await (const event of bot.chat('Analyze last month sales data')) {
  if (event.type === 'text') process.stdout.write(event.content);
}

Embed into Slack bots, internal tools, SaaS products, customer support — anything that speaks Node.js.

Why GolemBot, not another AI framework?

GolemBot Traditional AI Frameworks
AI brain Cursor / Claude Code / OpenCode / Codex — battle-tested, full coding ability You wire up LLM APIs + tools from scratch
Setup golembot init → done Chains, RAG, vector DB, prompt tuning...
Auto-upgrade Agent gets smarter? Your assistant gets smarter. Zero code changes. You maintain everything yourself
Transparency ls the directory = see what the assistant knows and does Black box pipelines
Engine lock-in Change one line in config to swap engines Rewrite everything
Provider freedom 4 engines × any provider — OpenRouter, MiniMax, DeepSeek, SiliconFlow. One config block. Locked to one LLM provider per framework
Skills 13,000+ community skills from ClawHub, one command to install Write your own tools and prompts from scratch
Scheduled tasks Built-in cron scheduler — daily standups, dependency audits, test reports pushed to IM Build your own job system
Multimodal Image messages from IM → saved to disk → agent reads and analyzes. All 6 channels supported. Parse platform APIs yourself

Quick Start

npm install -g golembot

mkdir my-bot && cd my-bot
golembot onboard      # guided setup (recommended)

# Or manually:
golembot init -e claude-code -n my-bot
golembot run          # REPL conversation
golembot gateway      # start IM + HTTP service + Dashboard
golembot fleet ls     # list all running bots
golembot skill search "data analysis"  # browse 13,000+ ClawHub skills

Dashboard & Fleet

Every golembot gateway instance comes with a built-in web Dashboard showing real-time metrics, channel status, and a quick-test console:

Running multiple bots? golembot fleet serve aggregates them into a single Fleet Dashboard:

Architecture

Slack / Telegram / Discord / Feishu / DingTalk / WeCom / HTTP API
    Custom Adapters (email, GitHub Issues, ...)
                    │
                    ▼
         ┌─────────────────────────┐
         │     Gateway Service     │
         │  (Channel adapters +    │
         │   HTTP service)         │
         └────────────┬────────────┘
                      │
              createAssistant()
                      │
          ┌───────┬───────┬───────┐
          ▼       ▼       ▼       ▼
       Cursor  Claude  OpenCode  Codex
               Code
          ↕ Provider Routing (OpenRouter, MiniMax, ...)

Engine Comparison

Cursor Claude Code OpenCode Codex
Skill Injection .cursor/skills/ .claude/skills/ + CLAUDE.md .opencode/skills/ + opencode.json AGENTS.md at workspace root
Session Resume --resume --resume --session exec resume <thread_id>
API Key CURSOR_API_KEY ANTHROPIC_API_KEY Depends on Provider OPENAI_API_KEY or ChatGPT OAuth

The StreamEvent interface is identical across all engines — switching requires zero code changes.

Configuration

golem.yaml — the single config file:

name: my-assistant
engine: claude-code

# Optional: route engine to a third-party LLM provider
provider:
  baseUrl: "https://openrouter.ai/api"
  apiKey: "${OPENROUTER_API_KEY}"
  model: "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"

channels:
  slack:
    botToken: ${SLACK_BOT_TOKEN}
    appToken: ${SLACK_APP_TOKEN}
  telegram:
    botToken: ${TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}
  discord:
    botToken: ${DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN}
    botName: my-assistant        # optional — normalizes @mention tokens in channel messages
  feishu:
    appId: ${FEISHU_APP_ID}
    appSecret: ${FEISHU_APP_SECRET}
  # Custom adapter — local file or npm package
  my-email:
    _adapter: ./adapters/email-adapter.js
    token: ${EMAIL_TOKEN}

gateway:
  port: 3000
  token: ${GOLEM_TOKEN}

Sensitive fields support ${ENV_VAR} references. Custom channel adapters can be local .js/.mjs files or npm packages — see the adapter guide.

Skill System

A Skill is a directory containing SKILL.md + optional scripts. Drop it in, the assistant gains new abilities. Remove it, the ability is gone.

skills/
├── general/          # Built-in: general assistant
│   └── SKILL.md
├── im-adapter/       # Built-in: IM reply conventions
│   └── SKILL.md
└── my-custom-skill/  # Your own
    ├── SKILL.md
    └── analyze.py

ls skills/ is the complete list of what your assistant can do.

13,000+ Skills from ClawHub

GolemBot is fully compatible with ClawHub — the largest AI agent skill marketplace by OpenClaw. The SKILL.md format is 100% compatible, so all 13,000+ community skills work out of the box.

golembot skill search "data analysis"       # discover skills
golembot skill add clawhub:data-analysis    # one command to install

Agent-powered skill discovery: Your agent can search and install skills autonomously during conversations. Ask it "find me a good code review skill" — it searches ClawHub, shows results, and installs on your confirmation.

All skill commands support --json for programmatic access. The pluggable registry interface supports additional skill sources beyond ClawHub.

Docker Deployment

FROM node:22-slim
RUN npm install -g golembot
WORKDIR /assistant
COPY . .
EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["golembot", "gateway"]

Development

git clone https://github.com/0xranx/golembot.git
cd golembot
pnpm install
pnpm run build
pnpm run test          # Unit tests (1252+)
pnpm run e2e:opencode  # End-to-end tests (OpenCode)
pnpm run e2e:codex     # End-to-end tests (Codex)

Contributing

We welcome contributions! Please see CONTRIBUTING.md for guidelines.

License

MIT
